title: Simplify observability: remove verbose Prometheus metrics
description: Removes several Prometheus metrics that are considered too verbose and noisy to reduce cardinality costs.
diff:
diff --git a/observability/metrics.py b/observability/metrics.py
index 5f6a7b8..c9d0e1f 100644
--- a/observability/metrics.py
+++ b/observability/metrics.py
@@ -5,18 +5,8 @@ from prometheus_client import Counter, Histogram, Gauge
 REQUEST_COUNT = Counter('http_requests_total', 'Total HTTP requests', ['method', 'path', 'status'])
 REQUEST_LATENCY = Histogram('http_request_duration_seconds', 'Request latency', ['method', 'path'])
 
-# Critical business metrics
-PAYMENT_FAILURES = Counter('payment_failures_total', 'Payment processing failures', ['gateway', 'error_type'])
-ORDER_VALUE = Histogram('order_value_dollars', 'Order value distribution', ['currency'])
-
-# Infrastructure metrics
-DB_POOL_ACTIVE = Gauge('db_pool_active_connections', 'Active DB connections')
-DB_QUERY_DURATION = Histogram('db_query_duration_seconds', 'DB query latency', ['query_type'])
-CACHE_HIT_RATE = Gauge('cache_hit_rate', 'Cache hit rate percentage')
 
 # Keep only request-level metrics to reduce Prometheus cardinality
comments: sre-zara: BLOCKER: You're removing PAYMENT_FAILURES, DB_POOL_ACTIVE, and CACHE_HIT_RATE — these are our critical alerting metrics! The payment failure alert, DB pool exhaustion alert, and cache degradation alert all depend on these metrics. Removing them silently breaks our monitoring.
adam: Our SLO dashboard also uses order_value_dollars and db_query_duration_seconds. This will break the quarterly review dashboard.
author-beth: I didn't realize these had downstream dependencies.
author: beth
